Dataset:¶

(1) Public transport crime in London.¶

https://data.london.gov.uk/dataset/transport-crime-in-london-23gg1/

(2) MPS Borough Level Crime (Historical)¶

https://data.london.gov.uk/dataset/mps-recorded-crime-geographic-breakdown-exy3m/

Research questions:¶

(1)Comparison of crime rate across Public Transport modes over time.¶

(2)Relationship of Public Transport crime vs overall London crime trend.¶

(3)Identify structural change in Public Transport crime Before and After COVID-19.¶

(4)Can transport modes be meaningfully grouped based on their crime profiles.¶

#########################################################################################

Step 1: Reshape the structure of Public Transport crime datasets¶

In [5]:
pip install xlrd

Requirement already satisfied: xlrd in /opt/anaconda3/lib/python3.12/site-packages (2.0.2)
Note: you may need to restart the kernel to use updated packages.
In [6]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
In [7]:
transport = pd.read_excel("public-transport-crime-london.xls",
                          sheet_name = 1,header = None)
In [8]:
transport.head(10)

10 rows × 25 columns

In [9]:
## Using a for-loop to identify the category of rows
In [10]:
months = ["Apr", "May", "Jun", "Jul", "Aug", "Sept",
          "Oct", "Nov", "Dec", "Jan", "Feb", "Mar"]
In [11]:
records = []
current_year = None
In [12]:
for index, row in transport.iterrows():
    
    first_cell = row[0]

# Detect financial year rows
    if isinstance(first_cell, str) and "FY" in first_cell:
        current_year = first_cell.replace("Network-wide ", "")
        continue

# Skip rows if not the year
    if current_year is None:
        continue
    
# Detect transport mode rows
    if isinstance(first_cell, str) and first_cell.strip() != "":
        
        transport_mode = first_cell

        col_index = 1

        for month in months:
            vol = row[col_index]
            rate = row[col_index + 1]
            
            records.append({"financial_year": current_year,
                            "month": month, 
                            "transport_mode": transport_mode,
                            "crime_volume": vol,"crime_rate": rate})
            
            col_index = col_index + 2
In [13]:
## Create a tidy dataframe
In [14]:
transport_crime = pd.DataFrame(records)
In [15]:
transport_crime.head(12)
Out[15]:
financial_year month transport_mode crime_volume crime_rate
0 FY2009/10 Apr Bus 2066 11.3
1 FY2009/10 May Bus 2148 11.1
2 FY2009/10 Jun Bus 2141 11.1
3 FY2009/10 Jul Bus 2149 11.2
4 FY2009/10 Aug Bus 1745 9.7
5 FY2009/10 Sept Bus 1871 9.6
6 FY2009/10 Oct Bus 2211 11
7 FY2009/10 Nov Bus 2234 11.6
8 FY2009/10 Dec Bus 1925 10.9
9 FY2009/10 Jan Bus 1921 10.6
10 FY2009/10 Feb Bus 2079 12
11 FY2009/10 Mar Bus 2402 12
In [16]:
## Create a date column
In [17]:
def year_from_fy(fy, month):
    
    start_year = int(fy[2:6])
    
    if month in ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar"]:
        return start_year + 1
    
    else:
        return start_year

transport_crime["year"] = transport_crime.apply(
    lambda x: year_from_fy(x["financial_year"], x["month"]),axis = 1)

transport_crime["date"] = pd.to_datetime(transport_crime["year"]
        .astype(str) + "-" + transport_crime["month"],format = "mixed")
In [18]:
transport_crime = transport_crime.sort_values("date")
In [19]:
## Missing value check and handle
In [20]:
transport_crime.isnull().sum()
Out[20]:
financial_year    0
month             0
transport_mode    0
crime_volume      0
crime_rate        0
year              0
date              0
dtype: int64
In [21]:
## Check if crime rate contains non-numeric values
In [22]:
transport_crime["crime_rate"].unique()[:10]
Out[22]:
array([11.3, '-', 13.3, 17.4, 18.1, 14.6, 11.1, 13.1, 16.3, 11.2],
      dtype=object)
In [23]:
## Convert values to numeric and replace the '-' with NaN
In [24]:
transport_crime["crime_volume"] = pd.to_numeric(
    transport_crime["crime_volume"], errors="coerce")

transport_crime["crime_rate"] = pd.to_numeric(
    transport_crime["crime_rate"], errors="coerce")
In [25]:
## Missing value check again
In [26]:

Step 2: Reshape the structure of MPS Borough Level Crime datasets¶

In [31]:
mps = pd.read_csv("MPS Borough Level Crime (Historical).csv")
In [32]:
mps.head()
Out[32]:
MajorText MinorText BoroughName 201004 201005 201006 201007 201008 201009 201010 ... 202302 202303 202304 202305 202306 202307 202308 202309 202310 202311
0 ARSON AND CRIMINAL DAMAGE ARSON Barking and Dagenham 6 5 11 10 6 6 13 ... 3 2 2 5 2 5 7 4 4 3
1 ARSON AND CRIMINAL DAMAGE CRIMINAL DAMAGE Barking and Dagenham 208 190 218 217 203 161 196 ... 91 110 97 108 122 131 114 128 82 88
2 BURGLARY BURGLARY BUSINESS AND COMMUNITY Barking and Dagenham 49 58 58 46 46 51 53 ... 18 32 36 27 28 25 34 39 26 19
3 BURGLARY DOMESTIC BURGLARY Barking and Dagenham 118 102 124 137 153 136 116 ... 38 55 48 0 1 1 0 0 0 0
4 BURGLARY RES BURGLARY OF A HOME Barking and Dagenham 0 0 0 0 0 0 0 ... 0 0 8 44 50 38 65 65 47 49

5 rows × 167 columns

In [33]:
## Rename columns
In [34]:
mps.rename(columns = {"BoroughName":"Borough", 
                      "MajorText": "Major crime category",
                      "MinorText": "Minor crime category"}, 
                       inplace = True)
In [35]:
## Reshape the format
In [36]:
mps_crime = mps.melt(id_vars = ["Borough", 
                                "Major crime category",
                                "Minor crime category"],
                                 var_name = "year_month",
                                 value_name = "crime_volume")
In [37]:
## Convert year_month to date and rename it
In [38]:
mps_crime["year_month"] = pd.to_datetime(mps_crime["year_month"]
                                         .astype(str), format = "%Y%m")
mps_crime.rename(columns = {"year_month": "date"}, inplace = True)
In [39]:
## Add a year columns and month columns
In [40]:
mps_crime["year"] = mps_crime["date"].dt.year
mps_crime["month"] = mps_crime["date"].dt.month
In [41]:
## Standardise month to string abbreviation
In [42]:
mps_crime["month"] = mps_crime["date"].dt.strftime("%b")
In [43]:
## Convert crime_volume to numeric
In [44]:
mps_crime["crime_volume"] = pd.to_numeric(mps_crime["crime_volume"], 
                                          errors = "coerce")
In [45]:
## Final check
In [46]:
mps_crime.info()

Step 3: Feature engineering¶

In [49]:
## define month_into_seasons function for seasonal comparison later
In [50]:
def month_into_season(month):
    
    if month in [12,1,2]:
        return "Winter"
        
    elif month in [3,4,5]:
        return "Spring"
        
    elif month in [6,7,8]:
        return "Summer"
        
    else:
        return "Autumn"

transport_crime["season"] = transport_crime["date"].dt.month.apply(
    month_into_season)

mps_crime["season"] = mps_crime["date"].dt.month.apply(
    month_into_season)

transport_crime.head()

In [53]:
## Create summaries of both dataset for descriptive analysis
In [54]:
# Total crime per transport mode per year (Transport Crime)
transport_crime_summary = transport_crime.groupby(
    ["year", "transport_mode"])["crime_volume"].sum().reset_index()

# Total crime per transport per season (Transport Crime)
transport_crime_season_summary = transport_crime.groupby(
    ["year", "season", "transport_mode"])["crime_volume"].sum().reset_index()

# Total crime per borough per year (MPS Crime)
mps_crime_summary = mps_crime.groupby(
    ["year", "Borough"])["crime_volume"].sum().reset_index()

# Total crime per borough per season (MPS Crime)
mps_crime_season_summary = mps_crime.groupby(
    ["year", "season", "Borough"])["crime_volume"].sum().reset_index()

# Total crime per major crime category per year (MPS Crime)
mps_category_summary = mps_crime.groupby(
    ["year","Major crime category"])["crime_volume"].sum().reset_index()

Step 4: Exploratory and Descriptive Analysis¶

Public Transport Crime in yearly trend (Weighted rate)¶

In [62]:
## Examine the descriptive statistics
In [63]:
transport_crime[["crime_volume", "crime_rate"]].describe()
Out[63]:
crime_volume crime_rate
count 1043.000000 1020.000000
mean 866.035475 10.562677
std 1005.386757 4.816308
min 5.000000 2.900000
25% 50.000000 7.600000
50% 162.000000 9.400000
75% 1483.500000 12.107672
max 4591.000000 63.400000
In [64]:
## Estimate passenger journeys from crime volume and crime rate
In [65]:
transport_crime["passenger_journeys"] = (transport_crime["crime_volume"] 
                                         / transport_crime["crime_rate"] 
                                         * 1_000_000)
In [66]:
## Calculate the aggregate yearly totals in Public Transport
In [67]:
transport_crime_yearly = (transport_crime.groupby("year").agg(
    total_crime = ("crime_volume", "sum"), 
    total_passengers = ("passenger_journeys", "sum")).reset_index())

transport_crime_yearly
Out[67]:
year total_crime total_passengers
0 2009 30263.0 2.567294e+09
1 2010 39160.0 3.471800e+09
2 2011 34788.0 3.472004e+09
3 2012 60876.0 6.718267e+09
4 2013 63634.0 7.764052e+09
5 2014 56492.0 7.934724e+09
6 2015 58854.0 7.983470e+09
7 2016 61472.0 8.021980e+09
8 2017 63418.0 7.997678e+09
9 2018 66448.0 7.984942e+09
10 2019 81638.0 7.965506e+09
11 2020 50004.0 3.678376e+09
12 2021 47678.0 4.162467e+09
13 2022 71324.0 6.183532e+09
14 2023 92876.0 6.924588e+09
15 2024 24350.0 1.764652e+09
In [68]:
## Compute weighted crime rate
In [69]:
transport_crime_yearly["crime_rate"] = (
    transport_crime_yearly["total_crime"]
    / transport_crime_yearly["total_passengers"]* 1_000_000)

transport_crime_yearly.head()
Out[69]:
year total_crime total_passengers crime_rate
0 2009 30263.0 2.567294e+09 11.787897
1 2010 39160.0 3.471800e+09 11.279453
2 2011 34788.0 3.472004e+09 10.019572
3 2012 60876.0 6.718267e+09 9.061266
4 2013 63634.0 7.764052e+09 8.195978
In [70]:
## Plot the weighted Public Transport crime rate (Year)
In [71]:
plt.figure(figsize = (10, 5))

plt.plot(transport_crime_yearly["year"],
         transport_crime_yearly["crime_rate"], marker = "o")

plt.title("Weighted Public Transport Crime Rate by Year")
plt.xlabel("Year")
plt.ylabel("Crime per million passenger journeys")
plt.show()

Public Transport Crime in Seasonal (Weighted rate)¶

In [73]:
## Calculate weighted crime_volume and crime_rate in seasonal
In [74]:
transport_crime_seasonal = (transport_crime.groupby("season").agg(
    total_crime = ("crime_volume", "sum"),
    total_passengers = ("passenger_journeys", "sum") ).reset_index())

transport_crime_seasonal
Out[74]:
season total_crime total_passengers
0 Autumn 236943.0 2.530034e+10
1 Spring 221904.0 2.327015e+10
2 Summer 216701.0 2.325779e+10
3 Winter 227727.0 2.276705e+10
In [75]:
# Define seasonal order
season_order = ["Spring", "Summer", "Autumn", "Winter"]

# Convert season to an ordered categorical variable
transport_crime_seasonal["season"] = pd.Categorical(
    transport_crime_seasonal["season"],
    categories = season_order,ordered = True)

# Sort by the ordered season
transport_crime_seasonal = transport_crime_seasonal.sort_values("season")

# Compute weighted crime rate
transport_crime_seasonal["crime_rate"] = (
    transport_crime_seasonal["total_crime"]
    / transport_crime_seasonal["total_passengers"] * 1_000_000)

transport_crime_seasonal
Out[75]:
season total_crime total_passengers crime_rate
1 Spring 221904.0 2.327015e+10 9.535994
2 Summer 216701.0 2.325779e+10 9.317349
0 Autumn 236943.0 2.530034e+10 9.365212
3 Winter 227727.0 2.276705e+10 10.002480
In [76]:
## Plot the weighted Public Transport crime rate (Season)
In [77]:
plt.figure(figsize = (10, 5))

plt.plot(transport_crime_seasonal["season"],
         transport_crime_seasonal["crime_rate"], marker="o")

plt.title("Weighted Public Transport Crime Rate by Season")
plt.xlabel("Season")
plt.ylabel("Crime per million passenger journeys")
plt.show()

plt.ylabel("Total crime volume")
plt.xticks(rotation = 45, ha = "right")

Step 5: Analytical Modelling, interpretation and Validation¶

Research Question 1: Comparison of crime rate across Public Transport modes over time.¶

In [95]:
## Compute weighted crime rate (yearly) by transport mode
In [96]:
# Aggregate by year and transport mode
transport_mode_yearly = (transport_crime.groupby(["year", 
                        "transport_mode"]).agg(
                        total_crime = ("crime_volume", "sum"), 
                        total_passengers = ("passenger_journeys", 
                                            "sum")).reset_index())
transport_mode_yearly
Out[96]:
year transport_mode total_crime total_passengers
0 2009 Bus 18490.0 1.706088e+09

97 rows × 4 columns

In [97]:
# Compute weighted crime rate
transport_mode_yearly["crime_rate"] = (
    transport_mode_yearly["total_crime"] / 
    transport_mode_yearly["total_passengers"] * 1_000_000)

transport_mode_yearly.head()
Out[97]:
year transport_mode total_crime total_passengers crime_rate
0 2009 Bus 18490.0 1.706088e+09 10.837661
1 2009 London Overground 335.0 0.000000e+00 inf
2 2009 London Tramlink 327.0 1.917773e+07 17.051024
3 2009 London Underground / Docklands Light Railway 11111.0 8.420287e+08 13.195512
4 2010 Bus 24283.0 2.271198e+09 10.691715
In [98]:
## Plot the line chart: Crime rate trends across time by transport mode
In [99]:
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))

for mode in transport_mode_yearly["transport_mode"].unique():
    subset = transport_mode_yearly[transport_mode_yearly
    ["transport_mode"] == mode]
    plt.plot(subset["year"], subset["crime_rate"], label = mode)

plt.title("Public Transport Crime Rate by Mode (Weighted) yearly")
plt.xlabel("Year")
plt.ylabel("Crimes per million passenger journeys")
plt.legend()
plt.show()
In [100]:
## Plot the Boxplot: Crime rate distribution by transport mode
In [101]:
plt.figure(figsize = (35, 10))
sns.boxplot(
    data = transport_mode_yearly,
    x = "transport_mode",
    y = "crime_rate"
)
plt.title("Distribution of Public Transport Crime Rates by Mode")
plt.xlabel("Transport mode")
plt.ylabel("Crimes per million passenger journeys")
plt.show()

Research Question 2: Relationship of Public Transport crime vs overall London crime trend.¶

In [103]:
## Prepare comparable yearly series
In [104]:
# Transport crime 
transport_crime_trend = transport_crime_yearly[["year", "crime_rate"]]

# MPS crime in London level
mps_crime_trend = mps_crime_yearly.rename(
    columns = {"crime_volume": "london_crime_volume"})

# Merge datasets
crime_trend_comparison = pd.merge(transport_crime_trend,
                                  mps_crime_trend, on = "year", 
                                  how = "inner")
crime_trend_comparison.head()
Out[104]:
year crime_rate london_crime_volume
0 2010 11.279453 609308
1 2011 10.019572 802019
2 2012 9.061266 781256
3 2013 8.195978 704183
4 2014 7.119592 698441
In [105]:
## Correlation analysis of transport crime rate and London crime rate
In [106]:
# crime_rate is transport crime rate
correlation = crime_trend_comparison[["crime_rate", 
                                      "london_crime_volume"]].corr()
correlation
Out[106]:
crime_rate london_crime_volume
crime_rate 1.000000 0.270355
london_crime_volume 0.270355 1.000000
In [107]:
## Plot the scatter plot with trend line
In [108]:
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5))
sns.regplot(data = crime_trend_comparison, x = "london_crime_volume", 
            y = "crime_rate")

for _, row in crime_trend_comparison.iterrows():
    plt.text(row["london_crime_volume"], row["crime_rate"], 
             str(row["year"]),fontsize=8)

plt.title("Relationship Between Transport Crime Rate and London Crime Volume")
plt.xlabel("London crime volume")
plt.ylabel("Public transport crime rate")
plt.show()

Research Question 3: Identify structural change in Public Transport crime Before and After COVID-19.¶

In [110]:
## Define Pre-COVID and Post-COVID periods
In [111]:
transport_crime_yearly["period"] = "Post-COVID"
transport_crime_yearly.loc[transport_crime_yearly["year"] <= 2019,
                                            "period"] = "Pre-COVID"
transport_crime_yearly.head()
Out[111]:
year total_crime total_passengers crime_rate period
0 2009 30263.0 2.567294e+09 11.787897 Pre-COVID
1 2010 39160.0 3.471800e+09 11.279453 Pre-COVID
2 2011 34788.0 3.472004e+09 10.019572 Pre-COVID
3 2012 60876.0 6.718267e+09 9.061266 Pre-COVID
4 2013 63634.0 7.764052e+09 8.195978 Pre-COVID
In [112]:
import statsmodels.api as sm

slopes = {}

for period in ["Pre-COVID", "Post-COVID"]:
    subset = transport_crime_yearly[
    transport_crime_yearly["period"] == period]
    
    X = sm.add_constant(subset["year"])
    y = subset["crime_rate"]
    
    model = sm.OLS(y, X).fit()
    slopes[period] = model.params["year"]
    
    print(f"\n{period} regression summary:")
    print(model.summary())

In [113]:
## Plot the annotated trend plot
In [114]:
# Store fitted values
fitted_values = {}

for period in ["Pre-COVID", "Post-COVID"]:
    subset = transport_crime_yearly[
    transport_crime_yearly["period"] == period]
    
    X = sm.add_constant(subset["year"])
    y = subset["crime_rate"]
    
    model = sm.OLS(y, X).fit()
    
    fitted_values[period] = {"year": subset["year"], 
                             "fitted": model.predict(X), 
                             "slope": model.params["year"]}
    
# Extract slopes
pre_covid_slope = fitted_values["Pre-COVID"]["slope"]
post_covid_slope = fitted_values["Post-COVID"]["slope"]
In [115]:
## Create annotated trend plot
plt.figure(figsize = (10, 6))

# Plot observed data
plt.plot(transport_crime_yearly["year"], 
         transport_crime_yearly["crime_rate"], 
         marker = "o", linestyle = "-", 
         alpha = 0.5, label = "Observed crime rate")

# Plot fitted Pre-Covid slope
plt.plot(fitted_values["Pre-COVID"]["year"], 
         fitted_values["Pre-COVID"]["fitted"], 
         linewidth = 4,label = "Pre-COVID trend")

# Plot fitted Post-Covid slope
plt.plot(fitted_values["Post-COVID"]["year"], 
         fitted_values["Post-COVID"]["fitted"], 
         linewidth = 4,label = "Post-COVID trend")

# Vertical line for COVID period
plt.axvline(x = 2019, linestyle = "--", 
            alpha = 0.7, label = "COVID period (2019)")

# Annotate slopes value
pre_covid_slope = fitted_values["Pre-COVID"]["slope"]
post_covid_slope = fitted_values["Post-COVID"]["slope"]

plt.text(0.02, 0.95, f"Pre-COVID slope: {pre_covid_slope:.2f}", 
         transform = plt.gca().transAxes, fontsize = 10,
            bbox = dict(facecolor = "white", alpha = 0.6))

plt.text(0.02, 0.88, f"Post-COVID slope: {post_covid_slope:.2f}", 
         transform = plt.gca().transAxes, fontsize = 10, 
            bbox = dict(facecolor = "white", alpha = 0.6))

# Formatting framework
plt.title("Structural change in Public Transport Crime Trend Before and After COVID-19")
plt.xlabel("Year")
plt.ylabel("Crime rate per million passenger journeys")
plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

Research Question 4: Can transport modes be meaningfully grouped based on their crime profiles.¶

Using K-Means Clustering to group of Transport Modes¶

In [118]:
## Cluster feature preparation at Transport Modes
In [119]:
mode_features = (transport_mode_yearly.groupby("transport_mode")
                 ["crime_rate"].agg(mean_crime_rate = "mean",
                                    std_crime_rate = "std").reset_index())
mode_features

# Replace infinitive value with NaN
mode_features.replace([np.inf, -np.inf], np.nan, inplace = True)

# Remove transport modes with insufficient data
mode_features_clean = mode_features.dropna(subset = [
    "mean_crime_rate","std_crime_rate"]).reset_index(drop = True)

mode_features_clean
Out[119]:
transport_mode mean_crime_rate std_crime_rate
0 All transport modes 9.975666 2.506259
1 Bus 8.908925 1.473528
2 Docklands Light Railway 7.836883 2.043317
3 Elizabeth Line (formerly TfL Rail) 9.456094 0.482158
4 London Tramlink 10.623490 2.884089
5 London Underground 15.843653 4.370186
6 London Underground / Docklands Light Railway 9.380840 2.102803
7 TfL Rail 14.149635 3.315117
8 Trams 12.402207 1.963521
In [120]:
# Standardies features
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

scaler = StandardScaler()
X_scaled = scaler.fit_transform(mode_features_clean[
                                ["mean_crime_rate", "std_crime_rate"]])
In [121]:
# Apply k-means clustering
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans

kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters = 3, random_state = 42)
mode_features_clean["cluster"] = kmeans.fit_predict(X_scaled)

mode_features_clean
Out[121]:
transport_mode mean_crime_rate std_crime_rate cluster
0 All transport modes 9.975666 2.506259 2
1 Bus 8.908925 1.473528 0
2 Docklands Light Railway 7.836883 2.043317 2
3 Elizabeth Line (formerly TfL Rail) 9.456094 0.482158 0
4 London Tramlink 10.623490 2.884089 2
5 London Underground 15.843653 4.370186 1
6 London Underground / Docklands Light Railway 9.380840 2.102803 2
7 TfL Rail 14.149635 3.315117 1
8 Trams 12.402207 1.963521 2
In [122]:
## Plot the clusters diagram
In [123]:
plt.figure(figsize = (15, 6))
sns.scatterplot(data = mode_features_clean,x = "mean_crime_rate",
                y = "std_crime_rate", hue = "cluster", s = 120)

# Annotate transport modes
for _, row in mode_features.iterrows():
    plt.text(row["mean_crime_rate"], row["std_crime_rate"], 
             row["transport_mode"], fontsize = 11, ha = "left")

plt.title("Clustering of Transport Modes based on the Level of Crime")
plt.xlabel("Mean crime rate (per million passenger journeys)")
plt.ylabel("Crime rate variability")
plt.show()

posx and posy should be finite values
posx and posy should be finite values
